summary: Documentation-only change: adds bench/ac/RESULTS.md, a provisional SF=1 access-controlled-query benchmark results doc, following the established bench/*/RESULTS.md pattern (ast-compact, fo-km, pkg-dogfood, compose, terse). Verified against the checkout: bench/ac/run.sh exists and supports --sf with the W1/W2-oracle/W3 oracle lanes, live W2/W4 lanes, and the anti-vacuity check; GenParams::smoke() has seed 42 (crates/sparq-acbench/src/lib.rs:111); the over-share (HARD fail-closed) vs under-share (advisory) vs ACP group-oracle divergence vs consortium ODRL temporal-embargo divergence distinctions the doc describes are all documented in live/src/main.rs and src/main.rs; ODRL live gating on the odrl-bridge feature matches run.sh. Internal arithmetic is fully consistent: all four column totals (150/383/172/753/15) sum correctly; every expressibility row (native/expansion/approximation/unsupported) sums to that use case's intent count and the per-model totals 161/0/0/11, 132/31/0/9, 172/0/0/0 reconcile; the timing table's derived W4/W2 per-check ratios are self-consistent (implied W2:W4 check ratio matches across WAC and ACP within each use case, a strong signal the numbers were computed not fabricated). Honesty rules respected: every timing value is explicitly labeled non-canonical work-box indicative, absolute values are disclaimed as not a performance claim, and canonical measurement is stated as still gated on the quiet EC2 runner (#1364). No code, tests, or security surface touched; no blockers or majors found.
